definitions: When two or more people or groups are working towards different or conflicting goals, often unintentionally, leading to inefficiency or failure.
To have conflicting aims or intentions that hinder effective collaboration.
examples: The two departments were working at cross-purposes, which is why the project was delayed.
We need to clarify our objectives, otherwise, we'll just be working at cross-purposes.
Their efforts were wasted because they were working at cross-purposes.
